title: EHR Production Fleet Snapshot — what's actually deployed at hospitals
description: Per-vendor summary of CapabilityStatement + smart-configuration harvested from every endpoint listed in the vendor's SMART STU 2.2 / HTI-1 §170.404 brands & endpoints registry. v1 of The Map measured one sandbox per vendor; v2 (this artifact) measures the production fleet — typically hundreds to thousands of customer deployments running the same vendor software with different configurations. Raw per-endpoint evidence lives under tests/golden/production-fleet/{ehr}/{captured_date}/. This summary is the rolled-up view the synthesizer joins onto the per-EHR Map view.

ehr.description: Lowercase identifier matching the parent ehrs/{ehr}/ directory.

captured_date.description: ISO date the harvest ran. Quarterly cadence is the target.

brands_bundle_source_url.description: URL or path of the brands bundle the endpoint list was drawn from (e.g., https://open.epic.com/Endpoints/R4 or tests/golden/cross-vendor/{stem}.json). For multi-bundle vendors (e.g., Oracle Health's provider + patient host fronts) this is a newline-delimited list of paths.

brands_bundle_total_endpoints.description: Number of Endpoint resources in the brands bundle at capture time.

failure_categories.description: Count of failures grouped by reason (dns, tls, timeout, http_4xx, http_5xx, parse, other)

software_distribution.description: Histogram of CapabilityStatement.software.{name + version} across reachable endpoints. Reveals patch-level deployment drift across the fleet.

fhir_version_distribution.description: Histogram of CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion. Should be uniform within a brands bundle but isn't always.

capstmt_shape_clusters.description: Endpoints clustered by the SHAPE of their CapStmt — sorted JSON of rest[0].resource[*].{type, supportedProfile, searchParam name list, interaction code list, security.service code list}. Customers in the same cluster expose identical-shape FHIR APIs.

cluster_id.description: e.g. 'epic-cluster-A' (ranked by size, A = largest). For multi-bundle vendors the prefix encodes the harvest origin: 'cerner-cluster-A' for the provider tier, 'cerner-patient-cluster-A' for the patient tier.

modal.description: True if this is the largest cluster within its access scope — the 'reference deployment' for the vendor (or for that access tier)

access_scope.description: Optional. Set on multi-bundle vendors (e.g., Oracle Health) to record which host front this cluster was harvested from. 'provider' = system/EHR-launch tier (fhir-ehr.cerner.com); 'patient' = standalone patient-launch tier (fhir-myrecord.cerner.com).

shape_hash.description: sha256 of the canonicalized shape JSON

example_endpoint_id.description: An endpoint slug from this cluster (anchors the per-cluster HTML page)

endpoints.description: Full roster of hospital endpoints in this cluster. Includes the modal cluster (which historically only surfaced its example_endpoint), so consumers can render 'hospitals in this cluster' for every cluster, not just outliers.

npi.description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier. Resolved from CMS NPD endpoint→Organization join (authoritative when present) with NPPES name+state fallback for the long tail. See identity_sources for provenance.

ccn.description: CMS Certification Number, from POS hospital catalog name+address Jaccard. Authoritative for hospitals; absent for non-facility providers.

pac_id.description: PECOS Associate Control ID. Stable across NPI changes — useful as a longitudinal join key. Filled from CMS PPEF enrollment extract when the endpoint's NPI is enrolled in Medicare.

state.description: 2-letter state. Provenance order: NPD Org.address > POS hospital record > NPPES practice location.

taxonomy.description: NUCC Healthcare Provider Taxonomy Code (e.g., '282N00000X' = Acute Care Hospital). Currently sourced from NPPES; characterizes the org's primary specialty/facility type.

medicare_provider_type.description: CMS-canonical Medicare provider-type description from PECOS PPEF (e.g., 'HOSPITAL', 'FQHC', 'AMBULATORY SURGICAL CENTER'). Complementary to NUCC taxonomy: this one's the Medicare billing category, taxonomy is the clinical specialty. Only populated when the endpoint's org NPI is actively enrolled in Medicare.

parent_org_name.description: From NPD OrganizationAffiliation hierarchy. Picks the most-recognizable system name (e.g., 'AdventHealth' over 'AdventHealth Tampa-FL Facility 22').

identity_sources.description: Which overlay sources contributed to this endpoint's identity. Order reflects layering: npd / pos / nppes / pos_via_nppes / pecos. Useful for citation and confidence inspection.

state_distribution.description: Top states represented in this cluster, derived from per-endpoint state attribution.

recognizable_members.description: Up to 10 distinct organization names in this cluster, ranked so a cluster page headline reads like names a clinician would recognize: (1) NPD-catalogued parents first (strongest 'these facilities roll up to this system' signal), (2) names repeating across endpoints, (3) singletons ranked by brand-quality heuristic (penalize legal-entity suffixes like LLC/PA/INC, reward healthcare keywords Hospital/Medical Center/Children's, reward mixed-case curated names). Tied entries break alphabetically. endpoint_count is the distinct count of endpoints in this cluster that reference the name (as parent_org_name or managing_organization_name).

smart_config_drift.description: Cross-customer drift in .well-known/smart-configuration. Reveals where vendor 'guidance' diverges from actual deployment.

grant_types_supported_distribution.description: Histogram of grant_types_supported as a sorted JSON-encoded set

us_core_profile_support_rate.description: Per US Core profile: how many reachable customers list it in any rest[0].resource[].supportedProfile. The headline 'production reality' finding — vendor sandbox claims X profiles but only Y% of customers actually advertise each one.

profile_id.description: Profile id (last URL segment). May be us-core-* (in baseline) OR a vendor / non-baseline profile observed in the fleet (e.g., 'pediatric-weight-for-height', vendor StructureDefinitions). The in_us_core_baseline flag distinguishes.

customers_with_capstmt.description: Denominator: customers whose CapStmt was successfully fetched

absent_in_modal_cluster.description: True if the modal cluster's CapStmt does NOT list this profile (= deployment-time omission, not patient-data gap)

outlier_endpoints.description: Endpoints whose CapStmt cluster is in the long tail (NOT the modal cluster). Worth surfacing because integrators hitting these customers will see different shapes than the vendor sandbox advertised.

managing_organization_name.description: Null when the brands bundle has no Organization (e.g., Epic-R4 ships Endpoint-only) or when the Organization reference is missing/unresolvable.

deviation_summary.description: Brief human-readable summary of how this endpoint diverges from modal

source_url.description: Path to the per-vendor harvest directory under tests/golden/production-fleet/
